name: binwalk3
title: binwalk3

commands.name: binwalk3

arguments.description: Search for all signatures at all offsets
arguments.raw: -a, --search-all             Search for all signatures at all offsets

arguments.description: Plot the entropy of the specified file
arguments.raw: -E, --entropy                Plot the entropy of the specified file

arguments.description: Log JSON results to a file
arguments.raw: -l, --log <LOG>              Log JSON results to a file

arguments.description: Manually specify the number of threads to use
arguments.raw: -t, --threads <THREADS>      Manually specify the number of threads to use

arguments.description: Do no scan for these signatures
arguments.raw: -x, --exclude <EXCLUDE>...   Do no scan for these signatures

arguments.description: Extract files/folders to a custom directory [default: extractions]
arguments.raw: -C, --directory <DIRECTORY>  Extract files/folders to a custom directory [default: extractions]

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# binwalk3 -h
 Analyzes data for embedded file types
 
 Usage: binwalk3 [OPTIONS] [FILE_NAME]
 
 Arguments:
 [FILE_NAME]  Path to the file to analyze
 
 Options:
 -L, --list                   List supported signatures and extractors
 -q, --quiet                  Supress output to stdout
 -v, --verbose                During recursive extraction display *all* results
 -e, --extract                Automatically extract known file types
 -M, --matryoshka             Recursively scan extracted files
 -a, --search-all             Search for all signatures at all offsets
 -E, --entropy                Plot the entropy of the specified file
 -l, --log <LOG>              Log JSON results to a file
 -t, --threads <THREADS>      Manually specify the number of threads to use
 -x, --exclude <EXCLUDE>...   Do no scan for these signatures
 -y, --include <INCLUDE>...   Only scan for these signatures
 -C, --directory <DIRECTORY>  Extract files/folders to a custom directory [default: extractions]
 -h, --help                   Print help
 -V, --version                Print version

apt_metadata.depends: libc6 (>= 2.39), libfontconfig1 (>= 2.12.6), libfreetype6 (>= 2.4.2), libgcc-s1 (>= 4.2), liblzma5 (>= 5.1.1alpha+20120614), sasquatch
apt_metadata.description:
tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
Binwalk is a tool for identifying, and optionally extracting, files and data
that have been embedded inside of other files.
.
While its primary focus is firmware analysis, it supports a wide variety of
file and data types.
.
Through entropy analysis, it can even help to identify unknown compression or
encryption!
.
Binwalk can be customized and integrated into your own Rust projects
.
This is an updated version of the Binwalk firmware analysis tool, re-written
in Rust for speed and accuracy.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
